AAPL.name: Apple Inc.
AAPL.business: Designs and sells consumer electronics, software, and services. iPhone is the primary revenue driver.
AAPL.value_source: Ecosystem lock-in, brand premium, services recurring revenue (App Store, iCloud, Apple Music).
AAPL.what_breaks_it: iPhone demand plateau, regulatory forced sideloading, margin compression in services, China supply disruption.
AAPL.capital_pattern: Asset-light hardware with high services attach. Returns capital aggressively via buybacks.
AAPL.recent_structural: Services segment growing faster than hardware. Vision Pro optionality. India manufacturing diversification.

MSFT.name: Microsoft Corporation
MSFT.business: Enterprise software, cloud computing (Azure), productivity tools (Office 365), and gaming (Xbox).
MSFT.value_source: Azure hyperscaler compounding, Office 365 sticky recurring revenue, GitHub developer platform.
MSFT.what_breaks_it: Azure losing share to AWS/GCP, OpenAI dependency risk, regulatory antitrust action on cloud bundling.
MSFT.capital_pattern: High ROIC software business. Balanced capital return with M&A (Activision, Nuance).
MSFT.recent_structural: Copilot AI integration across all products. OpenAI partnership. Azure Arc hybrid push.

NVDA.name: NVIDIA Corporation
NVDA.business: Designs GPUs and AI chips. Dominant in AI training accelerators (H100, Blackwell). Also gaming and automotive.
NVDA.value_source: CUDA software moat, data center AI monopoly, NIM microservices ecosystem.
NVDA.what_breaks_it: Custom silicon competition (Google TPU, Amazon Trainium, Meta MTIA), export restrictions to China, hyperscaler in-house chips.
NVDA.capital_pattern: Fabless model. Extreme margin expansion from data center mix shift. Minimal fixed assets.
NVDA.recent_structural: Blackwell ramp. NIM platform launch. China export controls creating revenue gap.

AMZN.name: Amazon.com Inc.
AMZN.business: E-commerce marketplace, AWS cloud, advertising, Prime subscription, logistics network.
AMZN.value_source: AWS ROIC engine subsidizes retail. Advertising growing as high-margin attach. Prime flywheel.
AMZN.what_breaks_it: AWS losing hyperscaler share, FTC antitrust break-up risk, logistics cost inflation, retail margin pressure.
AMZN.capital_pattern: Reinvests aggressively. AWS cash cow funds retail and new bets (Kuiper, healthcare, robotics).
AMZN.recent_structural: Generative AI infusion in AWS (Bedrock, Q). Aggressive logistics capacity build. Advertising scaling.

GOOGL.name: Alphabet Inc. (Class A)
GOOGL.business: Search advertising, YouTube, Google Cloud, Android, hardware, and autonomous vehicles (Waymo).
GOOGL.value_source: Search monopoly with 90%+ share. YouTube advertising growth. GCP accelerating.
GOOGL.what_breaks_it: AI-powered search disruption (ChatGPT/Perplexity), antitrust forced search divestiture, cloud market share loss.
GOOGL.capital_pattern: High-ROIC core funds moonshots. Buybacks increasing. DeepMind/Waymo optionality.
GOOGL.recent_structural: Gemini integration. DOJ antitrust search remedies pending. Cloud winning AI deals.

META.name: Meta Platforms Inc.
META.business: Social media advertising (Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp). Reality Labs (VR/AR). AI infrastructure.
META.value_source: 3B+ user advertising network. Instagram Reels competing with TikTok. WhatsApp monetization upside.
META.what_breaks_it: Regulatory ad targeting restrictions, TikTok competition stealing time, teen user decline, Reality Labs burn.
META.capital_pattern: High-margin advertising funding massive capex in AI and metaverse. Buybacks ongoing.
META.recent_structural: AI-driven ad personalization improving ROI. Llama open-source AI. Threads as Twitter alternative.

TSLA.name: Tesla Inc.
TSLA.business: Electric vehicles, energy storage, solar, and autonomous driving (FSD). Robotics (Optimus).
TSLA.value_source: EV brand leadership, software-defined vehicle margin, FSD license revenue potential, energy business.
TSLA.what_breaks_it: EV price war eroding margins, FSD regulatory delay, Chinese competition (BYD), execution on Optimus.
TSLA.capital_pattern: Manufacturing-intensive with software optionality. Low debt. Vertical integration from battery to software.
TSLA.recent_structural: Model Y refresh. Cybertruck ramp. FSD subscription growth. Chinese market pressure.

JPM.name: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
JPM.business: Diversified banking: consumer banking, commercial banking, investment banking, asset management.
JPM.value_source: Best-in-class deposit franchise, capital markets leadership, technology investment advantage over peers.
JPM.what_breaks_it: Interest rate compression, credit cycle deterioration, capital requirement increases (Basel III endgame), recession.
JPM.capital_pattern: Returns capital via buybacks and dividends when capital exceeds regulatory minimums.
JPM.recent_structural: AI integration across banking. First Republic acquisition integration. Investment banking recovery.

V.name: Visa Inc.
V.business: Global payments network. Earns on every card transaction processed on its network worldwide.
V.value_source: Network effects (issuers + merchants), brand trust, global acceptance infrastructure.
V.what_breaks_it: Disintermediation by real-time payment rails (FedNow, UPI), BNPL reducing card spend, antitrust.
V.capital_pattern: Asset-light toll road. 50%+ margins. Returns nearly all FCF via buybacks and dividends.
V.recent_structural: Value-added services (Visa DPS, consulting) growing. Tap-to-pay penetration. B2B payment push.

MA.name: Mastercard Incorporated
MA.business: Global payments network. Earns on transactions, cross-border fees, and value-added services.
MA.value_source: Network effects, brand, global acceptance. Cross-border premium fees are high-margin.
MA.what_breaks_it: Same as Visa: real-time payment substitution, BNPL, antitrust. Plus geopolitical isolation risk.
MA.capital_pattern: Asset-light. Very high ROIC. Aggressive buybacks. Similar to Visa but more international exposure.
MA.recent_structural: Crypto settlement work. Open Banking via Finicity. Click-to-Pay penetration.

BRK.B.name: Berkshire Hathaway Inc. (Class B)
BRK.B.business: Diversified conglomerate: insurance (GEICO), railroad (BNSF), utilities, manufacturing, and major public equity holdings.
BRK.B.value_source: Insurance float deployed as free capital. BNSF and BHE are durable infrastructure. Equity portfolio alpha.
BRK.B.what_breaks_it: Succession uncertainty, insurance catastrophe losses, concentrated Apple position, regulatory utility constraints.
BRK.B.capital_pattern: Float-funded equity compounding. No dividend. Buybacks when price below intrinsic value.
BRK.B.recent_structural: Cash pile near record high. Japan trading house positions. GEICO profitability recovery.

LLY.name: Eli Lilly and Company
LLY.business: Pharmaceutical company. GLP-1 weight loss and diabetes drugs (Mounjaro, Zepbound) driving most growth.
LLY.value_source: GLP-1 patent protection through 2030s, pipeline depth (Alzheimer's, obesity), manufacturing scale.
LLY.what_breaks_it: GLP-1 patent cliff, manufacturing constraint limiting supply, Medicare drug price negotiation.
LLY.capital_pattern: R&D-intensive. Growing dividend. Capex surge for GLP-1 manufacturing capacity.
LLY.recent_structural: Zepbound approved for OSA and heart failure. Oral GLP-1 pipeline. Donanemab Alzheimer's filing.

UNH.name: UnitedHealth Group Incorporated
UNH.business: Largest US health insurer (UnitedHealthcare) plus health services platform (Optum).
UNH.value_source: Optum integrated care delivery flywheel, data advantage, employer/government payer relationships.
UNH.what_breaks_it: MLR (medical loss ratio) spikes, government reimbursement cuts, Change Healthcare disruption fallout.
UNH.capital_pattern: High ROIC compounder. Consistent buybacks and dividend growth. Optum acquiring physician groups.
UNH.recent_structural: CEO departure following assassination. Change Healthcare cyberattack impact. DOJ antitrust scrutiny of Optum.

XOM.name: Exxon Mobil Corporation
XOM.business: Integrated oil and gas: upstream production, downstream refining, chemicals, and LNG.
XOM.value_source: Low-cost Permian Basin production, Pioneer acquisition synergies, refining margin leverage.
XOM.what_breaks_it: Oil price collapse below $60/bbl, energy transition accelerating demand destruction, capital return sustainability.
XOM.capital_pattern: Grows dividend through the cycle. Pioneer acquisition doubled Permian inventory.
XOM.recent_structural: Pioneer acquisition closed. Carbon capture projects. Chemical growth from Beaumont expansion.

JNJ.name: Johnson & Johnson
JNJ.business: Pharmaceutical and medical devices after spinning off consumer health (Kenvue). MedTech and Innovative Medicine.
JNJ.value_source: Durable pharma pipeline (immunology, oncology), MedTech procedure recovery, dividend king status.
JNJ.what_breaks_it: Talc/asbestos litigation, key drug LOEs (Stelara biosimilars), pipeline approval failures.
JNJ.capital_pattern: Consistent dividend grower (60+ years). Balanced buybacks. MedTech acquisitions.
JNJ.recent_structural: Stelara biosimilar competition beginning. Teclistamab, Talquetamab oncology launches. Kenvue spin complete.

WMT.name: Walmart Inc.
WMT.business: World's largest retailer. US stores, Sam's Club, international markets, and growing Walmart+ / advertising.
WMT.value_source: Everyday low price moat, logistics infrastructure, advertising segment (Walmart Connect) margin expansion.
WMT.what_breaks_it: Amazon grocery competition, inflation hitting consumer discretionary, operating leverage limits.
WMT.capital_pattern: Consistent dividend grower. Capex in automation and e-commerce. Advertising becoming high-margin attach.
WMT.recent_structural: Advertising and marketplace growing fast. Vizio acquisition for ad data. Flipkart India growth.
